mnemonics: DP B.C. ROD: Dynamic Programming uses Base Cases and Recursion/Overlapping Subproblems for Optimal Decomposition
keyPoints: Understand the difference between Greedy and Dynamic Programming approaches
Master recurrence relations and how to solve them (e.g., substitution, recursion trees, master theorem)
Be able to trace and analyze classic algorithms like Dijkstra, Kruskal, Floyd-Warshall, and Knapsack solutions
commonMistakes: Confusing when to apply Greedy (optimal substructure) vs. Dynamic Programming (overlapping subproblems)
Incorrectly setting up base cases or recurrence relations in DP
Misapplying the Master Theorem to recurrence relations that do not fit its standard form
